projects.name: Confidential Rural Visibility Review Pack
projects.heroLine: Drone mapping, 3D models, viewpoint review and interactive map layers for early-stage site reconnaissance.
projects.planningQuestion: Can the proposed poultry sheds be seen from surrounding viewpoints across the wider landscape?
projects.summary: An early-stage visibility review showing the site, surrounding landform and proposed poultry sheds in context, so likely visibility can be tested before any planning submission is prepared.
projects.decisionLine: This pack is set up as pre-planning reconnaissance: a practical check on where the site can be seen from, how clearly it reads in the landscape, and whether the more distant concerns are likely to be meaningful in reality.

metrics.label: Main Question
metrics.detail: Whether the proposed sheds are genuinely noticeable from surrounding roads, distant locations and other wider landscape views.

metrics.label: Evidence Included
metrics.detail: The pack combines the 3D scene, viewpoint visuals, map layers and panorama in one early-stage review route.

metrics.label: Evidence Route
metrics.detail: Review the 3D models first, then check each viewpoint image, the shed visuals, and the supporting map layers.

overviewSections.title: What This Pack Is For
overviewSections.body: This is an early-stage visibility review pack rather than a formal planning submission pack. It brings the drone survey, the 3D site model, the proposed shed model and the viewpoint images into one place so the likely visibility of the site can be tested quickly and clearly.

overviewSections.title: How To Read It
overviewSections.body: Start with the existing and proposed 3D models in the 3D Scene tab. Then use the Viewports tab to review the key comparison views and orientation map, and the Shed Visuals tab for detailed images of the proposed building. The Map Layers tab supports the review by showing the site layout, surface model and contours.

overviewSections.title: What The Images Will Show
overviewSections.body: The key question is not whether the site can be found with effort, but whether it would actually be noticed by someone passing through the landscape in normal conditions.

workflow: Survey the site and surrounding context by drone so the landform and wider landscape setting can be reviewed together.
Build the mapping outputs: orthomosaic for site layout, DSM for surface height, contours for landform, and a 360 panorama for context.
Place the proposed poultry sheds into the 3D site model using the best available design and position information.
Create matching existing and proposed images from the key roads, graveyard checks and longer-distance viewpoints across the valley.
Use this pack to review the findings in one clear route: model, viewpoints, supporting map layers and method.

viewpoints.name: Church-side Area
viewpoints.bearing: Looking towards the confidential rural site
viewpoints.note: Used to test the long-distance concern associated with the church side of the valley, rather than a confirmed residential receptor view.

viewpoints.name: Approach Lane
viewpoints.bearing: Looking across the farm entrance and proposed shed position
viewpoints.conclusion: Site can be seen but does not stand out
viewpoints.note: Useful for showing how the site reads from the road and how vegetation and landform interrupt the view.

viewpoints.name: Distant High Ground
viewpoints.bearing: Looking back towards the development platform
viewpoints.conclusion: Site remains recessive in the wider landscape
viewpoints.note: Useful for testing whether the site sits quietly in the landform rather than drawing attention at distance.

referenceViews.summary: Existing and proposed viewpoint images are arranged here as a simple visual comparison set for early-stage review.

views.title: Existing View 1
views.note: The proposed building sits quietly within the wider site with planting around it and does not draw attention in the view.

views.title: Existing View 2
views.note: From the road in area 2.2, you are looking towards the site, but other planting and greenery interrupt the view so the proposal does not stand out.

views.title: Existing View 3
views.note: The building sits back on the distant hilltop and does not stand out in the wider scene. It reads as part of the site with planting around it.

views.title: Existing View 4
views.note: This graveyard view looks towards the site, but I could not find anywhere in the church itself with a meaningful glimpse in this direction. The trees are not yet fully in leaf here.

views.title: Existing View 5
views.note: This is another graveyard view towards the site. As with View 4, there was no meaningful outward view from the church itself, and the tree cover shown here is before full leaf.

views.title: Existing View 6
views.note: This was the clearest place to see the site from, slightly down the road from the church area. Even here it is so far away that the development would be very unlikely to be noticed without deliberately looking for it.

twinmotion.summary: Supporting proposed images for hotspot linking and quick review alongside the main viewpoint comparisons.

method.surveyBase: The site has been captured by drone and processed into mapping and 3D outputs. These outputs provide the base material for understanding the site layout, surrounding landform and wider landscape setting.
method.existingModel: The existing model shows the surveyed site without the proposed poultry sheds. It is used as the baseline context for understanding what is currently visible in the landscape.
method.proposedModel: The proposed model places the poultry sheds into the same surveyed scene so their likely presence can be reviewed in context before any formal application stage.
method.viewpointRenders: The pack includes proposed viewpoint images from selected camera positions. These should be read alongside the orientation map so the direction of view and relationship to the site are clear.
method.mapLayers: The orthomosaic shows the site from above, the DSM helps explain surface height and landform, and the contour layer helps show how the landscape may screen or expose the proposed buildings.
method.materialAssumptions: The poultry shed materials are treated as matte and realistic, because overly reflective finishes can make buildings appear more prominent than they would be in practice.
method.interpretation: The important question is not simply whether the site can be found in an image, but whether it would actually be noticed by someone moving through the landscape in ordinary use.
method.limitations: This pack is an early-stage review and reconnaissance tool. If it is later developed into formal planning material, the viewpoint positions, distances, shed dimensions, model placement, imagery dates and any rendering assumptions should all be confirmed.
